type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
his facility (13-25) is reporting NOx, CO, VOC, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2014. This facility reported NOx, CO, VOC, PM10 and PM2.5 emissions for 2013. The following changes occurred from 2013 to 2014, respectively:
1) NOx emissions increased by 119%
2) CO emissions increased by 116%
3) VOC emissions increased by 115%
4) PM10 emissions increased by 70%
5) PM2.5 emissions increased by 70%
Potential sources of these differences are:
1) Added new equipment in July 2013, which was operational for the entire year in 2014.
2) Added new equipment in November 2014: two (2) new 1,000 hp Waukesha compressor engines, one (1) new Caterpillar 1,480 hp compressor engine, one (1) new boiler, and 34 new heaters.
3) Flaring volume increased from 457.7 e3m3 to 757.9 e3m3.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The NOx emissions calculations have been reviewed for 2020. We confirm that the reported quantities calculated based on the 2020 Petrinex data as well as equipment specs and operating hours provided by Tourmaline are correct.
Emissions factors were taken from either engine manufacturer’s specification sheets, engine tests, or published documents by Clearstone Engineering Ltd.
